other_observers: none; I was not the original finder of this bird. Numerous other people had previously reported (via cobirds) seeing it.
description: The bird was with American Wigeons on a pond.  It had a similar size and shape to the American Wigeons.  The bill was bluish gray.  The face was a uniform dark reddish brown. The eye (or eye patch) was darker, blackish in color.  The forehead and crown were a golden color.  The breast was a dull reddish gray, mottled.  The back and sides were gray, with white wing patches visible.  The flanks were a clean white.  The tail and the base of the tail were black.Behaviors: swimming in a pond with other puddle ducks (including many American Wigeon)Call: none heardPlumage: adult basic plumage
